dataSourceInstructions:
# Renewable Energy Data Source Instructions

Use gold-layer tables for renewable energy analytics. Preserve existing SQL schema names and column keys. Translate business wording as follows: customer means offtaker or utility buyer, product means renewable product or PPA product, order means energy delivery or settlement commitment, invoice means energy settlement invoice, and payment means settlement payment.

Ground every answer in available fields. If users ask for metrics that are not modeled, such as asset availability percentage, curtailment MWh, REC serial numbers, balancing authority, interconnection queue position, emissions factors, or real-time generation telemetry, explain that those fields are not present in this demo datasource.
userDescription:
# Renewable Energy Gold Tier Data Lake

This datasource exposes synthetic renewable energy portfolio data in the Microsoft Fabric gold lakehouse. Stable schema, table, and column keys are preserved from the accelerator so existing notebooks, semantic model bindings, reports, and Data Agent SQL examples continue to work.

## Domain interpretation

- `shared.customer`: synthetic offtakers, utility buyers, municipal buyers, and corporate PPA buyers.
- `shared.customerrelationshiptype`: synthetic PPA, REC, interconnection, storage tolling, community solar, and asset ownership relationship categories.
- `shared.product` and `shared.productcategory`: renewable products such as solar PPA, wind PPA, REC bundles, storage services, capacity attributes, green tariff products, and curtailment settlement examples.
- `salesfabric` and `salesadb`: renewable energy delivery commitments and settlement line items. `Quantity` represents delivered MWh, REC count, storage MWh, MW-month, or curtailment settlement quantity based on `ProductName`.
- `finance`: energy settlement accounts, invoices, and payments.

All rows are synthetic demonstration records. Do not treat locations, settlement values, or product names as real operational, financial-control, trading, grid, or compliance data.

Synthetic regional renewable operating areas; not real asset or critical infrastructure locations.
Synthetic PPA, REC, interconnection, community solar, storage, and ownership relationship categories.
Synthetic delivered quantity, REC, storage, capacity, or curtailment settlement line items.
Synthetic renewable energy delivery or generation commitment settlement headers.
